customManagers.description: Match container image repository+tag pairs in HelmRelease values (optional @sha256 digest pin, e.g. tag: dev@sha256:... so Renovate tracks rolling-tag digest updates)
Track vendored GitHub-release manifests via a manifest-version sentinel comment (e.g. cnpg-barman-plugin)

packageRules.description: Group Flux toolkit updates into one PR
Use loose versioning for Plex (non-semver tags)
Use loose versioning for Linuxserver images, but only accept semver-style tags
Ignore latest and testing tags — pin to a real version to get updates
app-template updates get their own PR (affects every app)

plugin-barman-cloud is tracked via the manifest-version sentinel in its kustomization.yaml. Suppress the image-tag-only PR that Renovate's default kubernetes manager would otherwise raise from the vendored manifest.yaml.

Hold pvc-plumber on v3.x. v4 collapses Kyverno-admission + oracle into a single operator and drops the HTTP-only /exists endpoint our Kopia ClusterPolicy depends on. See memory project_pvc_plumber_v4_planned_upgrade.md for the migration scope before flipping this off.

dumb is pinned to the rolling :dev tag by digest (tag: dev@sha256:...). There is no semver to bump, so track digest changes: Renovate raises a PR each time the dev image is rebuilt. Review/merge to upgrade deliberately instead of pulling latest dev on every restart.

bazarr is pinned to the linuxserver :development rolling tag by digest. That floating tag silently reverted to an older build (2026-06-04), breaking a DB migration on restart. Track digest changes so Renovate raises a PR to review/test before adopting a new dev build.

hermes (nousresearch/hermes-agent) is pinned to the rolling :latest tag by digest (tag: latest@sha256:...). The generic latest-ignore rule above would suppress ALL updates including digest bumps, so the in-cluster backend silently goes stale and the Hermes Desktop client reports 'backend out of date'. Re-enable digest tracking so Renovate raises a PR each time latest is rebuilt; review/merge to adopt the new backend deliberately.

pocket-bridge (self-built, ghcr.io/mainertoo/pocket-bridge) is pinned to :latest by digest (tag: latest@sha256:...). The pocket-bridge-image.yaml workflow rebuilds :latest on every src/** merge to master, but the generic latest-ignore rule suppresses the digest bump — so the deployed image silently goes stale (the PR #1139 probe/annotation fix landed in src but only reached the cluster once the digest was hand-bumped). Track digest changes so Renovate raises a PR each rebuild; review/merge to deploy.
